description: Tool disambiguation contracts — source of truth for descriptions and benchmarks

velixar_context.use_when: broad orientation needed, starting a task, unclear what is relevant
velixar_context.do_not_use_when: you know the exact entity or have a specific search query

velixar_search.use_when: specific factual assertions, past decisions, known topic

velixar_inspect.use_when: deep inspection of a specific memory by ID
velixar_inspect.do_not_use_when: broad search without a known ID

velixar_graph_traverse.use_when: relationships, dependencies, connections from a known entity
velixar_graph_traverse.do_not_use_when: broad topic briefing with no focal entity

velixar_timeline.do_not_use_when: broad context with no temporal question

velixar_contradictions.use_when: conflict suspected or surfaced by another tool

velixar_identity.use_when: understanding who the user is, preferences, expertise

velixar_patterns.use_when: current problem may match prior patterns

velixar_distill.do_not_use_when: transient chatter or single explicit facts

velixar_session_resume.use_when: resuming work from a previous session

velixar_batch_search.use_when: multiple independent queries needed, separate results per query
velixar_batch_search.do_not_use_when: need merged/deduplicated results (use multi_search) or single lookup

velixar_batch_store.do_not_use_when: single fact (use store) or session distillation (use distill)

velixar_act.use_when: non-trivial change that future sessions will need to know about (architectural decision, hardening work, multi-file edit, design doc)
velixar_act.do_not_use_when: trivial lookups, conversational turns, pure read-only commands
velixar_act.status: forward-declared — workflow lives in the recall_first prompt until the server-enforced wrapper ships (see RECALL-FIRST-MODE-TASKS.md Section 5 Step 6)
